title: Wittelsbachia orinocensis
taxonomy: Wittelsbachia orinocensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Venezuela. The classification of Wittelsbachia orinocensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Asterales. Within the family Asteraceae, it belongs to the tribe Heliantheae. Wittelsbachia orinocensis is a rare and endemic species found in the Andes Mountains.

intro: Wittelsbachia orinocensis, also known as the Orinoco wattle, is a flowering plant species native to the savannas and dry forests of the Guayana Shield in South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help in the treatment of various conditions including wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related diseases.
